statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 18 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages above minimum thresholds.
fullAnalysis:
The EU's fuel security picture shows concerning fragmentation across member states, with widespread critical shortfalls despite aggregate reserves appearing adequate. While EU averages of 112.8 days for petrol, 101.5 days for diesel, and 95.1 days for jet fuel all exceed the 90-day minimum, this masks severe vulnerabilities at the national level. Romania faces the most acute crisis with all three fuel types in critical status, including diesel reserves at just 30.2 days — one-third of the required minimum.

Jet fuel emerges as the most problematic category, with 18 of 27 countries holding critical or warning-level reserves. This includes major economies like Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days), alongside smaller states like Poland (25.3 days) and Portugal (26.7 days). The aviation fuel shortage reflects both increased demand recovery and supply chain constraints following recent strategic reserve releases due to Middle East disruptions.

Fuel prices continue climbing alongside crude oil, which has surged 46.7% since late February to $106.3/barrel. The Netherlands leads with petrol at €2.389/L, while significant price disparities persist across the bloc — Malta pays nearly €1 less per liter than the Netherlands. This price pressure, combined with inventory strains, suggests the EU remains vulnerable to further supply shocks despite recent efforts to diversify energy sources.
keyPoints: 18 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania experiencing critical shortfalls across all three fuel types
Jet fuel shows the most widespread vulnerability, with reserves critically low in countries including Germany (64.7 days), France (67.5 days), and Poland (25.3 days)
Oil prices have surged 46.7% since late February to $106.3/barrel, driving fuel costs higher with petrol ranging from €1.34/L in Malta to €2.389/L in the Netherlands
Major economies face uneven risk profiles — Germany has safe petrol stocks (100.7 days) but critical diesel and jet fuel reserves
Eastern European countries show particular strain, with Poland and Romania among the most vulnerable despite their strategic importance for regional energy security
